title: Access Office for Budget Responsibility Data
text: Office Budget Responsibility UK’s independent fiscal watchdog. created 2010 coalition government provide independent check government’s fiscal plans - role previously held HM Treasury . distinction matters. HM Treasury government department sets fiscal policy: decides tax rates, spending plans, much government intends borrow. OBR’s job scrutinise plans independently, producing economic fiscal forecasts influenced ministers. Think equivalent Bank England’s independence monetary policy, applied public finances. practice, means OBR publishes forecasts Budget Autumn Statement showing whether thinks government track meet fiscal rules - political incentive optimistic.

title: Access Office for Budget Responsibility Data
code: install.packages("obr")  # Or install the development version from GitHub # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("charlescoverdale/obr")

title: Access Office for Budget Responsibility Data
text: package covers five datasets listed .

title: Access Office for Budget Responsibility Data
text: OBR data freely available obr.uk. problem available: Excel files non-standard layouts, inconsistent headers, footnote-laden sheets require significant wrangling usable R. example, Public Finances Databank column headers buried row 4 spreadsheet, data starting row 8, trailing footnote numbers appended column names. Historical Forecasts Database stores forecasts vintage matrix - rows fiscal events, columns fiscal years - needs reshaping long format can plotted analysed. package handles automatically. One function call returns clean, tidy data frame. Data cached locally subsequent calls instant.
code: # Without this package path <- "~/Downloads/Public_finances_databank_March_2025.xlsx" raw  <- readxl::read_excel(path, sheet = "Aggregates (£bn)", col_names = FALSE) series_names <- as.character(unlist(raw[4, ])) # ... 30 more lines of wrangling ...  # With this package library(obr) get_psnb()

title: Access Office for Budget Responsibility Data
text: Every data-returning function returns obr_tbl: data.frame attached metadata recording OBR publication, publication vintage, source URL, data retrieved, MD5 fingerprint underlying spreadsheet. provenance prints automatically header data. Use obr_provenance() extract metadata list, summary() full provenance card. Provenance survives [ subsetting stripped explicit .data.frame(). means analysis pinned obr_tbl always carries audit trail OBR publication produced numbers.
code: library(obr) get_efo_fiscal() #> # obr_tbl: <rows> x <cols> #> # Source:       OBR Economic and Fiscal Outlook, <vintage> #> # URL:          https://obr.uk/download/<vintage-slug>/ #> # Retrieved:    <timestamp> #> # File MD5:     <md5 prefix> #> # Package:      obr <version> #> #> <data rows ...> psnb <- get_psnb() obr_provenance(psnb) # Returns a list with: publication, vintage, source_url, # retrieved (POSIXct), file_md5, package_version, notes

what: 1. How much did COVID cost the UK?
title: Access Office for Budget Responsibility Data
text: UK borrowed £318bn 2020-21 - roughly seven times pre-pandemic level - fund furlough, bounce-back loans, emergency NHS spending.

what: 2. How has the OBR’s borrowing forecast changed over time?
title: Access Office for Budget Responsibility Data
text: OBR first forecast 2024-25 borrowing £37bn (March 2022). November 2025, estimate risen £149bn - four times original figure. get_forecasts() function returns every published forecast across fiscal events, making straightforward visualise forecast drift assess fiscal plans evolved.
code: psnb_fc <- get_forecasts("PSNB") fc_2425 <- psnb_fc[psnb_fc$fiscal_year == "2024-25", c("forecast_date", "value")] fc_2425 #>    forecast_date   value #>     March 2022    36.5 #>  November 2022    84.3 #>     March 2023    85.4 #>  November 2023    84.6 #>     March 2024    87.2 #>   October 2024   127.5 #>     March 2025   137.3 #>  November 2025   149.5

what: 3. Has the UK ever run a surplus - and how likely is it to again?
title: Access Office for Budget Responsibility Data
text: UK last ran surplus 2000-01. 24 years since, government borrowed every year. Combine get_forecasts("PSNB_pct") see whether OBR projects future surpluses.

what: 4. Where does government revenue come from?
title: Access Office for Budget Responsibility Data
text: Income tax, VAT, National Insurance together account around 60% government receipts. Breaking time reveals long-run shifts - rising share income tax fiscal drag pulls earners higher bands.
code: receipts <- get_receipts()  # Top tax sources in 2023-24 r <- receipts[receipts$year == "2023-24", ] r <- r[order(-r$value), ] head(r[, c("series", "value")], 8) #>                              series   value #>   Public sector current receipts   1101.5 #>                       Income tax    290.4 #>                              VAT    183.1 #>    National insurance contributions 182.4 #>                    Corporation tax   88.4 #>                       Council tax   44.9 #>                         Fuel duty   24.5 #>                    Stamp duties     18.4

what: 5. What does the March 2026 Budget forecast for borrowing?
title: Access Office for Budget Responsibility Data

what: 7. What happens to the state pension bill as the UK ages?

title: Access Office for Budget Responsibility Data
text: Public Finances Databank accessed via stable URL OBR keeps pointed latest file. Historical Official Forecasts Database, EFO, WTR, FSR functions use dynamic URL resolver probes OBR’s recent fiscal events (recent first) uses whichever URL live, package automatically picks new editions without code change. resolver find live URL (example temporary network outage, OBR moved unfamiliar slug pattern), falls back last-known-good URL emits explicit warning rather failing silently. returned obr_tbl always records URL actually used, analysis carries audit trail. OBR publishes roughly predictable schedule: EFO twice year (March October/November), FSR summer, WTR annually. package’s fallback URLs refreshed release; check NEWS current fallback edition.

what: Get OBR policy measures by fiscal event — get_policy_measures
title: Get OBR policy measures by fiscal event — get_policy_measures
text: Downloads (caches) OBR Policy Measures Database, single workbook lists every tax spending measure scored UK fiscal event, Exchequer effect GBP million year forecast horizon. Tax measures cover 1970 date; spending measures cover 2010 date.

title: Get OBR policy measures by fiscal event — get_policy_measures
code: get_policy_measures(   type = c("tax", "spending"),   search = NULL,   since = NULL,   refresh = FALSE )

title: Get OBR policy measures by fiscal event — get_policy_measures
text: type Character vector. measures return: "tax", "spending", (default). search Optional character. regular expression used filter measure head (case-insensitive). example, search = "alcohol" keeps measure whose description head mentions alcohol. since Optional fiscal-year cut-(e.g. "2020-21"). Rows whose fiscal_year earlier dropped. refresh Logical. TRUE, re-download even cached copy exists. Defaults FALSE.

title: Get OBR policy measures by fiscal event — get_policy_measures
text: obr_tbl columns: event Fiscal event scored measure, e.g. "Budget 2024" (character) measure Plain-English description measure (character) head Tax head (tax measures) spending head (character) fiscal_year Fiscal year scored, e.g. "2024-25" (character) value_mn Exchequer effect GBP million. tax measures, positive value raises revenue; spending measures, positive value increases spending (numeric)

what: obr: Access 'Office for Budget Responsibility' Data — obr-package
title: obr: Access 'Office for Budget Responsibility' Data — obr-package
text: Provides clean, tidy access data published 'Office Budget Responsibility' ('OBR'), UK's independent fiscal watchdog. Covers Public Finances Databank (outturn PSNB, PSND, receipts, expenditure since 1946), Historical Official Forecasts Database (every 'OBR' forecast since 2010), Economic Fiscal Outlook detailed forecast tables (five-year projections latest Budget), Welfare Trends Report (incapacity benefit spending caseloads), Fiscal Risks Sustainability Report (50-year state pension projections). returned objects carry provenance metadata recording source URL, publication vintage, retrieval time, file fingerprint, analyses can audited reproduced. Data downloaded 'OBR' first use cached locally subsequent calls. Data sourced 'OBR' website https://obr.uk.

title: obr: Access 'Office for Budget Responsibility' Data — obr-package
text: Maintainer: Charles Coverdale charlesfcoverdale@gmail.com

what: Get the current UK fiscal rules — obr_fiscal_rules
title: Get the current UK fiscal rules — obr_fiscal_rules
text: Returns data frame describing Charter Budget Responsibility fiscal rules currently force, encoded package time release. Numerical headroom rule shipped constant updated every fiscal event go stale within months; users derive headroom current EFO output get_efo_fiscal(), consult OBR's EFO press release relevant vintage.

title: Get the current UK fiscal rules — obr_fiscal_rules
code: obr_fiscal_rules()

title: Get the current UK fiscal rules — obr_fiscal_rules
text: data frame columns: rule Short rule code: "stability", "investment", "welfare_cap". metric metric rule binds . target_description Plain-English target. target_year_rule target year set Charter. direction_of_pass Sign convention headroom. source_charter Charter version defines rule. source_act Act Parliament backing rule.

title: Get the current UK fiscal rules — obr_fiscal_rules
text: current Charter (Autumn 2024, Autumn 2025 update) sets three numerical rules: Stability rule: current budget balance surplus target year. target year 5th forecast year rolls 3rd forecast year 2026-27 onwards. Investment rule: Public Sector Net Financial Liabilities (PSNFL) share GDP falling year--year target year. Welfare cap: AME-capped welfare spending statutory cap level target year. Budget Responsibility Act 2024 also adds non-numerical fiscal lock requiring OBR forecast fiscally significant measure.

title: Get the current UK fiscal rules — obr_fiscal_rules
code: obr_fiscal_rules() #>          rule #> 1   stability #> 2  investment #> 3 welfare_cap #>                                                                           metric #> 1                                               Current budget balance, % of GDP #> 2 Public Sector Net Financial Liabilities (PSNFL), % of GDP, year-on-year change #> 3                                     AME-capped welfare spending, GBP bn (cash) #>                                                   target_description #> 1            Current budget in balance or surplus by the target year #> 2    PSNFL as a share of GDP falling year-on-year by the target year #> 3 AME-capped welfare spending below the cap level by the target year #>                                                  target_year_rule #> 1           5th forecast year (rolls to 3rd from 2026-27 onwards) #> 2           5th forecast year (rolls to 3rd from 2026-27 onwards) #> 3 Set in nominal terms for a specific fiscal year by the Treasury #>                               direction_of_pass #> 1         current budget surplus larger -> pass #> 2        PSNFL/GDP falling more steeply -> pass #> 3 AME-capped spending further below cap -> pass #>                                                         source_charter #> 1 Charter for Budget Responsibility, Autumn 2024 (updated Autumn 2025) #> 2 Charter for Budget Responsibility, Autumn 2024 (updated Autumn 2025) #> 3 Charter for Budget Responsibility, Autumn 2024 (updated Autumn 2025) #>                                     source_act #> 1 Budget Responsibility Act 2024 (fiscal lock) #> 2 Budget Responsibility Act 2024 (fiscal lock) #> 3 Budget Responsibility Act 2024 (fiscal lock)

what: New: provenance metadata on every returned object
title: obr 0.3.0
text: data-returning functions now return obr_tbl object: data.frame attached provenance recording source URL, OBR publication code (“PFD”, “HFD”, “EFO”, “WTR”, “FSR”), publication vintage (e.g. “March 2026”), retrieval timestamp, file MD5 fingerprint, obr package version. lets users audit OBR publication produced number, reproduce analyses across new package OBR releases. Print method shows provenance header data. summary() returns full provenance card. New helper obr_provenance() extracts metadata list. Subsetting [ preserves class attributes; .data.frame() strips .

what: New: vintage layer for reproducible analysis
title: obr 0.3.0
text: obr_efo_vintages() returns structured table every EFO published since OBR created June 2010, publication dates URL slugs. obr_as_of(date) returns EFO current given calendar date. Useful reproducing analyses looked given point time. obr_pin(vintage) sets session-wide EFO vintage flows get_efo_fiscal() get_efo_economy(). obr_unpin() clears obr_pinned() returns current pin. get_efo_fiscal() get_efo_economy() now accept vintage = argument overrides pin downloads file specific EFO. Cached files vintage-tagged different vintages overwrite .

title: obr 0.3.0
text: get_policy_measures() provides programmatic access OBR’s Policy Measures Database, one row per fiscal-event-scored measure since 1970 (tax) 2010 (spending). Columns include fiscal event, plain- English description, Treasury head, fiscal year, Exchequer effect GBP million. Supports filters: type (“tax”, “spending”, ), search (regex description / head, case-insensitive), since (fiscal-year cut-). policy_measures_summary() aggregates long format net Exchequer effect event fiscal year. programmatic access PMD: OBR otherwise distributes single Excel workbook non-standard layout.

title: obr 0.3.0
text: obr_fiscal_rules() returns structured table three numerical rules current Charter Budget Responsibility (Autumn 2024, updated Autumn 2025): stability rule (current budget surplus), investment rule (PSNFL/GDP falling), welfare cap. metric, pass direction, target-year mechanics, source Charter / Act shipped data. Numerical headroom shipped constant changes every fiscal event; users derive current EFO output via [get_efo_fiscal()] read EFO press release.

what: New: Forecast Revisions Database and forecast panel
title: obr 0.3.0
text: get_forecast_revisions() provides programmatic access OBR’s Forecast Revisions Database, decomposes EFO--EFO change headline PSNB forecast policy, classifications one-offs, underlying (economic determinants) components. Available GBP billion per cent GDP. obr_forecast_panel() pivots long-format Historical Forecasts Database wide real-time panel: rows forecast vintages, columns fiscal years, cells forecast values. Mirrors OBR’s Forecast Evaluation Report lays forecast performance lets users read h-step-ahead forecast vintage diagonal.

title: obr 0.3.0
text: public API renamed removed. existing exports keep signatures return columns; difference returned frames now carry obr_tbl class provenance attributes.

title: obr 0.3.0
text: Historical Forecasts Database URL now resolved dynamically across recent fiscal events (previously hardcoded March 2025). dynamic URL resolution falls hardcoded fallback publication (EFO, WTR, FSR, HFD), package now emits explicit cli::cli_warn() instead failing silently. returned data frame records source = "fallback" callers can detect state. Excel sheet-name lookup now tolerates encoding changes via regex fallback. Series whose primary sheet name uses non-ASCII Pound symbol (PSNB, receipts, expenditure) fall back letter-pattern literal name missing. obr_resolve_url() now uses HEAD requests faster candidate probing captures redirect target Public Finances Databank vintage can recovered stable slug.

title: obr 0.3.0
text: parse_efo_output_gap() (sheet 1.14) now scans value column aligned numeric entries instead hardcoding column 3. parse_pension_projections() (FSR sheet C1.2) now detects sections via fuzzy matching “demographic” “triple lock” rather exact strings. Receipts column footnote stripping now removes 1-2 trailing digits preceded letter, period, whitespace, series legitimately ending digits corrupted.

title: obr 0.3.0
text: Em-dashes removed roxygen titles avoid encoding issues Windows. match.arg() now used series measure arguments. inst/CITATION reads version DESCRIPTION stays sync.

title: obr 0.2.5
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-14 EFO, Welfare Trends Report, Fiscal Sustainability Report download URLs now resolved dynamically, trying recent publication dates reverse chronological order. prevents functions breaking OBR publishes new editions. Hardcoded URLs retained fallbacks.

title: obr 0.2.4
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-17 Removed non-existent pkgdown URL DESCRIPTION.

title: obr 0.2.3
text: Examples now cache tempdir() instead user’s home directory, fixing CRAN policy compliance \donttest examples. Cache directory now configurable via options(obr.cache_dir = ...).

title: obr 0.2.2
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-12 Fix README URLs flagged CRAN incoming checks (301 redirects). Add “Databank” WORDLIST.

title: obr 0.2.1
text: Update WORDLIST domain-specific terms (EFO, FSR, WTR, CPIH, etc.)

title: obr 0.2.0
